channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: 22 BRIDGE:BREAK Flaws Expose Thousands of Lantronix and Silex Serial-to-IP Converters
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have identified 22 new vulnerabilities in popular models of serial-to-IP converters from Lantronix and Silex that could be exploited to hijack susceptible devices and tamper ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Surge in Bomgar RMM Exploitation Demonstrates Supply Chain Risk
item.description: The critical remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-1731) in the remote monitoring and management tool can be exploited to spread ransomware and compromise supply chains....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google Fixes Critical RCE Flaw in AI-Based Antigravity Tool
item.description: The prompt injection vulnerability in the agentic AI product for filesystem operations was a sanitization issue that allowed for sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
item.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted hi...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Aiding BlackCat Attacks in 2023
item.description: A third individual who was employed as a ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to conducting ransomware attacks against U.S. companies in 2023. Angelo Martino, 41, of Land O'Lakes, Florida, teamed ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Stopping Fraud at Each Stage of the Customer Journey Without Adding Friction
item.description: Fraud prevention and user experience don't have to be a tradeoff. IPQS shows how combining identity, device, and network signals stops fraud without adding friction. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UK probes Telegram, teen chat sites over CSAM sharing concerns
item.description: Ofcom, the United Kingdom's independent communications regulator, has launched an investigation into Telegram based on evidence suggesting it's being used to share child sexual abuse material (CSAM). ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 5 Places where Mature SOCs Keep MTTR Fast and Others Waste Time
item.description: Security teams often present MTTR as an internal KPI. Leadership sees it differently: every hour a threat dwells inside the environment is an hour of potential data exfiltration, service disruption, r...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: NGate Campaign Targets Brazil, Trojanizes HandyPay to Steal NFC Data and PINs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new iteration of an Android malware family called NGate that has been found to abuse a legitimate application called HandyPay instead of NFCGate. "The threa...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA flags new SD-WAN flaw as actively exploited in attacks
item.description: ​CISA has given U.S. government agencies four days to secure their systems against another Catalyst SD-WAN Manager vulnerability it flagged as actively exploited in attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chinese APT Targets Indian Banks, Korean Policy Circles
item.description: China is spying on India's financial sector, for some reason, and it's not putting much effort into it, judging by some stale TTPs....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: No Exploit Needed: How Attackers Walk Through the Front Door via Identity-Based Attacks
item.description: The cybersecurity industry has spent the last several years chasing sophisticated threats like zero-days, supply chain compromises, and AI-generated exploits. However, the most reliable entry point fo...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Actively exploited Apache ActiveMQ flaw impacts 6,400 servers
item.description: Nonprofit security organization Shadowserver found that over 6,400 Apache ActiveMQ servers exposed online are vulnerable to ongoing attacks exploiting a high-severity code injection vulnerability. [.....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google Patches Antigravity IDE Flaw Enabling Prompt Injection Code Execution
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a vulnerability in Google's agentic integrated development environment (IDE), Antigravity, that could be exploited to achieve code execution. The flaw, since ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Former ransomware negotiator pleads guilty to BlackCat attacks
item.description: 41-year-old Angelo Martino, a former employee of cybersecurity incident response company DigitalMint, has pleaded guilty to targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks in 2023. [.....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: NGate Android malware uses HandyPay NFC app to steal card data
item.description: A new variant of the NGate malware that steals NFC payment data is targeting Android users by hiding in a trojanized version of HandyPay, a legitimate mobile payments processing tool. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA Adds 8 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets April-May 2026 Federal Deadlines
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added eight new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, including three flaws impacting Cisco C...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: KelpDAO suffers $290 million heist tied to Lazarus hackers
item.description: State-sponsored North Korean hackers are likely behind the $290 million crypto-heist that impacted the KelpDAO DeFi project on Saturday. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: China's Apple App Store infiltrated by crypto-stealing wallet apps
item.description: A set of 26 malicious apps on Apple App Store impersonate popular wallets, such as Metamask, Coinbase, Trust Wallet, and OneKey, to steal recovery or seed phrases and drain them of cryptocurrency asse...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
item.description: Stolen OAuth tokens, which are at the root of these breaches, "are the new attack surface, the new lateral movement," a researcher notes....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Serial-to-IP Devices Hide Thousands of Old & New Bugs
item.description: The OT devices that translate machine talk into Internet-speak are riddled with vulnerabilities and more frequently targeted for attacks, researchers say....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: The Gentlemen ransomware now uses SystemBC for bot-powered attacks
item.description: A SystemBC proxy malware botnet of more than 1,570 hosts, believed to be corporate victims, has been discovered following an investigation into a Gentlemen ransomware attack carried out by a gang affi...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Seiko USA website defaced as hacker claims customer data theft
item.description: The Seiko USA website was defaced over the weekend, displaying a message from attackers claiming they stole its Shopify customer database and threatening to leak it unless a ransom is paid. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) Enables RCE via Malicious GGUF Model Files
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in SGLang that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution on susceptible systems. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-576...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft: Teams increasingly abused in helpdesk impersonation attacks
item.description: Microsoft is warning of threat actors increasingly abusing external Microsoft Teams collaboration and relying on legitimate tools for access and lateral movement on enterprise networks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: WhatsApp Leaks User Metadata to Attackers
item.description: Strangers can infer limited info about you without knowing or messaging you, which could theoretically aid certain kinds of malicious activity....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: The backup myth that is putting businesses at risk
item.description: Backups protect data, but don't keep your business running during downtime. Datto shows why BCDR is essential to keep operations running during ransomware and outages. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Vercel Hack, Push Fraud, QEMU Abused, New Android RATs Emerge & More
item.description: Monday’s recap shows the same pattern in different places. A third-party tool becomes a way in, then leads to internal access. A trusted download path is briefly swapped to deliver malware. Browser ex...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: British Scattered Spider hacker pleads guilty to crypto theft charges
item.description: A British man, believed to be the leader of the Scattered Spider cybercrime collective, has pleaded guilty in the United States to charges of wire fraud and aggravated identity theft. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft tests Windows Explorer speed, performance improvements
item.description: Microsoft is rolling out multiple File Explorer changes to Windows 11 users in the Insider program, including improvements to launch speed and performance. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
